commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5591b45cbb38f4cabf71437c2bd9c914c2b4eff8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a default auto-reply lightweight lane that disables tools for classifier-admitted small-talk messages, plus Codex notification-state regression coverage and shard assignment.

PR surface: Source +259, Tests +312. Total +571 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: a message like `hey, what's the weather tomorrow?` matches the broad greeting pattern, misses the current-info denylist, then runs with disableTools true; I did not run live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to repair the routing/runtime guards and provide latest-head real behavior proof; the remaining default-routing policy is not a safe single automated fix.

target.title: feat(auto-reply,codex): lightweight reply lane and Codex watchdog
target.body:
## Summary
- add a conservative lightweight reply lane for positively identified low-risk small-talk replies
- keep uncertain, current-info, tool/action, media/link, high-stakes, code/config, unresolved reply-target, and configured skill-filter messages on the full path
- keep Codex accepted/pre-item turns on the configured attempt timeout so valid startup/prewarm silence is not cut short
- assign the Codex attempt notification-state test to the explicit app-server attempt support shard

## What Problem This Solves
Normal conversational replies were paying the full setup and tool-wiring cost even when no tools were needed, while earlier versions of this PR risked over-admitting ambiguous text and shortening valid Codex pre-item silence. This version keeps the optimisation limited to obvious small talk and preserves the configured Codex attempt budget before substantive item-level progress.

## Why
- obvious small-talk replies should not pay the full setup/tool wiring cost when no tools are needed
- over-escalation remains harmless because borderline, current-info, or policy-constrained messages stay on the full agent path
- Codex accepted turns should not be aborted earlier than the configured attempt timeout while startup/prewarm or pre-item work may still be legitimately silent

## Evidence
- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw Telegram replies now have a positive-match lightweight lane for obvious small talk, and Codex accepted/pre-item turns keep the configured attempt timeout rather than a fixed shorter cap.
- Existing Mantis proof: native Telegram Desktop before/after proof for an earlier head showed a plain chat reply delivered on both main and the candidate: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93244#issuecomment-4816610857
- Latest contributor fix on this head: ambiguous/current-info text such as “who won the game last night?” stays on the full path, agent skill filters still block lightweight admission, and accepted Codex turns no longer override the attempt timeout before item-level progress.
- Local proof on latest head:
  ```bash
  pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.ts src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-timeouts.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "does not time out when turn progress arrives before turn/start returns"
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts -t "uses merged agent skill policy before admitting the lightweight lane"
  pnpm check:test-types
  ```
- Observed result after fix: formatting passed; lightweight lane tests passed; Codex notification-state tests passed; focused run-attempt timing test passed; get-reply skill-policy regression passed; test types passed.
- What is still pending: refreshed hosted GitHub checks and any refreshed Mantis/ClawSweeper verdict for the latest head.

## Tests
- `pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.ts src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-timeouts.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "does not time out when turn progress arrives before turn/start returns"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts -t "uses merged agent skill policy before admitting the lightweight lane"`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
